notes: Consumer-plus-business NT-kernel Windows release; XP Home, Pro, Media Center, Tablet PC Edition; most widely-deployed Windows release; end-of-support 2014

tech_name: Microsoft Windows XP 64-bit Edition / Windows XP Professional x64 Edition
notes: Microsoft 64-bit Windows XP; Itanium edition 2001, AMD64/x64 edition 2005; released in beta Feb 2004; limited driver/application support; displaced by Windows Vista/7 x64
Microsoft next-gen Windows code-named Longhorn during 2001-2005 development; shipped as Windows Vista Jan 30 2007 after multi-year delays; reset 2004; poorly-received at launch
AMD 64-bit extension of x86 (Opteron Apr 2003, Athlon 64 Sep 2003); Intel adopted as Intel 64 (EM64T) 2004; became dominant 64-bit desktop/server ISA
HP/Intel joint 64-bit VLIW architecture; limited adoption confined to HP-UX/NonStop; killed by AMD64; last shipment 2021
AMD first desktop 64-bit CPU (Sep 2003, K8 microarchitecture); brought AMD64 ISA to consumer desktops ahead of Intel EM64T
AMD server/workstation CPU; first AMD64/x86-64 chip (Apr 2003); briefly dominant in 2-4 socket x86 servers 2004-2006 before Intel Core microarchitecture
64-bit Linux distributions on x86_64 and Itanium; mainstream Linux x86_64 availability from 2001-2002; 64-bit-first server OS ahead of Windows x64
Cited by Kastner as what AMD customers actually care about — a folksy-voice reference to consumer-gaming priority over OS-architecture transitions
